message:
🐛 Fix dashboard data disappearing on refresh (#2774)

Root cause: when the page refreshes, the cache layer correctly loads
persisted data from SQLite. But then each card's fetcher runs
immediately and returns [] because the agent/backend haven't
reconnected yet. The empty array overwrites the cached data and gets
saved to SQLite, wiping the cache.

Fix: if a fetcher returns empty data (same shape as initialData) but
the cache already has real data, keep the cached data instead of
overwriting it. The auto-refresh interval will fetch fresh data once
the agent connects.

Affected cards: PredictiveHealth, HardwareHealth, PodIssues,
DeploymentStatus, and any card using useCachedData hooks with
fetchers that return [] when data sources are unavailable.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -568,6 +568,23 @@ class CacheStore<T> {
         return
       }
 
+      // Guard: if the fetcher returned empty data (same as initialData) but we
+      // already have cached data, keep the cache. This prevents refresh from
+      // wiping card data when the agent/backend hasn't connected yet.
+      // Fetchers return [] when both agent and backend are unavailable, which
+      // would overwrite perfectly good cached data with nothing.
+      const isEmptyResult = Array.isArray(newData) && (newData as unknown[]).length === 0
+        && Array.isArray(this.initialData) && (this.initialData as unknown[]).length === 0
+      if (isEmptyResult && hasCachedData) {
+        // Don't overwrite cache — treat as "no data available yet"
+        this.fetchingRef = false
+        this.setState({
+          isLoading: false,
+          isRefreshing: false,
+        })
+        return
+      }
+
       const finalData = merge && hasCachedData ? merge(this.state.data, newData) : newData
 
       await this.saveToStorage(finalData)
